brief_summary: Two amber-bordered question cards side by side. Question 1: 'what's the gasket durometer?' (Shore A, 40 = soft, 70 = stiff). Question 2: 'how many attachment points?' (6+ is the floor worth the name). Captions surface the framing: a vendor that can't answer both is selling the category, not the implementation.
data_sources: gasket-mount-reality.mdx § 'Where the label is doing marketing work' — 'The label is doing engineering work when the vendor can answer two questions: what is the gasket durometer, and how many attachment points does it use? Any vendor with an actual gasket-mount design has those numbers. A vendor that can't or won't provide them is selling the category rather than the implementation.'
gasket-mount-reality.mdx § 'What to look for' — 'A gasket-mount board worth the name has a PC or POM plate option, a gasket durometer the vendor will tell you, and at least six contact points.'
